description:
You are a FinOps analyst who has just finished reviewing three monthly cloud-cost export files that live in:

    /home/user/cloud_costs/aws_cost_report.csv
    /home/user/cloud_costs/azure_cost_report.csv
    /home/user/cloud_costs/gcp_cost_report.csv

  To reduce object-storage costs you must archive these CSVs into a single, compressed “cold-storage” bundle.

  Target archive:
    • Full path: /home/user/finops_backup.tar.gz
    • Compression: gzip (i.e. a “.tar.gz” archive)
    • Archive layout: the **root** of the archive must contain only the three CSV files and no directory prefixes.  
      ├── aws_cost_report.csv  
      ├── azure_cost_report.csv  
      └── gcp_cost_report.csv

  What to do (single-line terminal solution):
    1. Create the archive exactly as described.
    2. Immediately verify it by printing the archive’s table of contents to STDOUT.

  Expected STDOUT format that the automated grader will capture (three lines, no extra spaces, no directory names):

    aws_cost_report.csv
    azure_cost_report.csv
    gcp_cost_report.csv

  Notes & constraints:
    • Accomplish both the creation and the listing in **one** shell line (you may chain commands with “&&” or “;”).
    • Do **not** move or delete the original CSV files; only create the archive and emit the listing. 
    • Do not output anything except the three-line listing shown above—no headers, no prompts, no blank lines.
    • You do not need sudo/root privileges.
truth:
<!-- Pre-existing files and their minimal contents -->
  Directory: /home/user/cloud_costs
    ├── aws_cost_report.csv    (text: "aws,2023-09,123.45\n")
    ├── azure_cost_report.csv  (text: "azure,2023-09,67.89\n")
    └── gcp_cost_report.csv    (text: "gcp,2023-09,45.67\n")

  After correct completion the system must contain:
    1. /home/user/finops_backup.tar.gz
       • A valid gzip-compressed tar archive.
       • When listed with “tar -tzf /home/user/finops_backup.tar.gz” the exact output is:

aws_cost_report.csv
azure_cost_report.csv
gcp_cost_report.csv

       • No directory components may precede the file names inside the archive.
    2. The three original CSV files remain unchanged at their original paths.

  The grader will:
    • Check that /home/user/finops_backup.tar.gz exists and is a gzip file.
    • Run   tar -tzf /home/user/finops_backup.tar.gz
      and compare the output (including order and newlines) to the three-line list above.
name: task_000000_80573fde
